ACDCode.Description: Driving or operating a CMV under the influence of alcohol with BAC of at least .04 but not greater than .079
Driving or operating a motor vehicle under the influence of alcohol with BAC at or over .08
Driving or operating a motor vehicle under the influence of alcohol with BAC at or over .10
Driving or operating a motor vehicle under the influence of alcohol with BAC at _ _ (detail field required)
Refused to submit to test for alcohol - Implied Consent Law
Driving or operating a motor vehicle under the infiluence of alcohol or drugs
Driving or operating a motor vehicle under the infiluence of alcohol
Driving or operating a motor vehicle under the infiluence of drugs
Driving or operating a motor vehicle under the infiluence of alcohol and drugs
Driving or operating a motor vehicle under the infiluence of medication not intended to intoxicate
Driving or operating a motor vehicle while impaired
Drinking alcohol while driving or operating a motor vehicle
Illegal possession of alcohol
Illegal possession of drugs (controlled substances)
Possession of open alcohol container while driving or operating a motor vehicle
Driver violation of ignition interlock or immobilization device and/or lease agreement
Motor vehicle used in the commission of a felony involving manufacturing, distributing, or dispensing a controlled substance
Underage convicted of drinking, and driving or operating a motor vehicle over the lowest BAC level limit allowed by the jurisdiction
Underage Administrative Per Se - drinking, and driving or operating a motor vehicle over the lowest BAC level limit allowed by the jurisdiction
Administrative Per Se for BAC at or over .10
Administrative Per Se for BAC at _ _ (detail field required)
Administrative Per Se for BAC of at least .04 but not greater than .079 while driving or operating a CMV
Administrative Per Se for Drugs
Administrative Per Se for BAC at or over .08
Hit and run - failure to stop and render aid after crash
Hit and run - failure to stop and render aid after crash - Fatal crash
Hit and run - failure to stop and render aid after crash - Personal injury crash
Hit and run - failure to stop and render aid after crash - Property damage crash
Leaving crash scene before police arrive
Leaving crash scene before police arrive - Fatal crash
Leaving crash scene before police arrive - Personal injury crash
Leaving crash scene before police arrive - Property damage crash
Failure to reveal identity after fatal or personal injury crash

Driving or operating a CMV in violation of a driver or vehicle out-of-service order while transporting hazardous materials as defined in §383.5, or while driving or operating a vehicle designed to transport 16 or more passengers, including the driver
Driving or operating a motor vehicle while license withdrawn
Driving or operating a motor vehicle while license barred
Driving or operating a motor vehicle while license cancelled
Driving or operating a motor vehicle while license denied
Driving or operating a motor vehicle while license disqualified
Driving or operating a motor vehicle while license revoked
Driving or operating a motor vehicle while license suspended
Driving or operating a CMV in violation of a driver or vehicle out-of-service order (for violations not covered by B19)
Possess or provide counterfeit or altered driver license (includes DL, CDL, and Instruction Permit) or ID
Expired or no non-commercial driver license or permit
Driving or operating a CMV without obtaining a CLP or CDL
Driving or operating a CMV without a CLP or CDL in the driver's possession
Failed to file crash report
Failed to file future proof of financial responsibility
Failed to file insurance certification
Failed to file medical certification/disability information
Failed to show non-commercial driver license (includes Instruction Permit)

Driving or operating without the proper class and/or endorsement on commercial or non-commercial permit or license for the specific vehicle group being operated or driven, or for the passengers or type of cargo being transported
Misrepresentation of identity or other facts on application, including required self-certifications, for non-commercial permit or license
Misrepresentation of identity or other facts to obtain alcohol
Possess multiple driver licenses (includes DL, CDL, and Instruction Permit)
Manufacture or make false driver license (includes DL, CDL, and Instruction Permit)
Show or use improperly - Driver license (includes DL, CDL, and Instruction Permit) while driving or operating a motor vehicle
Violate limited license conditions
Violate restrictions of driver license (includes DL, CDL, and Instruction Permit)
Misrepresentation of identity or other facts on application, including required self-certifications, for CLP or CDL
Fraud related to the issuance of a CLP or CDL
Failure to comply with financial responsibility law
Failure to maintain required liability insurance
Failure to pay for damages or make installment payment
Failure to post security or obtain release from liability
Unsatisfied judgment
Failure to appear for trial or court appearance (detail sometimes required)
Failure to make required payment of child support
Failure to make required payment of fine and costs (detail sometimes required)
Failure to answer a citation, pay fines, penalties and/or costs related to the original violation (detail sometimes required)
Driver's view obstructed
Inability to control vehicle
Driving or operating a motor vehicle improperly because of drowsiness
Driving or operating a motor vehicle improperly due to physical or mental disability
Perjury about the operation of a motor vehicle
Operating without equipment as required by law
Operating without brakes as required by law
Operating without hazmat safety equipment as required by law
Operating without hazmat placards/markings as required by law
Operating without lights as required by law
Operating without school bus equipment as required by law
Use of radar or laser detector prohibited by law
Failure to use equipment as required
Failure to use brakes
Failure to use hazmat safety devices as required
Failure to use headlight dimmer as required
Failure to use lights as required
Failure to use school bus safety equipment as required
Failure to use snow tires or chains as required
Equipment used improperly or obstructed
Brakes used improperly
Child or youth restraint not used properly as required
Motorcycle safety equipment not used properly as required
Seat belt not used properly as required
Carrying unsecured passengers in open area of vehicle
Improper operation of or riding on a motorcycle
Stopping, standing, or parking: obstructing or impeding traffic with a motor vehicle
Failure to obey barrier
Failure to obey construction or maintenance zone markers
Failure to obey filagger
Failure to obey lane markings or signal
Failure to obey police or peace officer
For all individuals driving or operating a motor vehicle, failure to obey railroad-highway grade crossing restrictions not specifically noted in other railroad-highway grade crossing related codes.
For all individuals driving or operating a CMV, failure to obey a traffic control device or the directions of an enforcement official at a railroad-highway grade crossing.
Failure to obey restricted lane
Failure to obey safety zone
Failure to obey school crossing guard
Failure to obey sign or traffic control device
Failure to obey stop sign
Failure to obey traffic signal or light
Failure to obey traffic sign
Failure to obey warning light or filasher
Failure to obey yield sign
For individuals driving or operating a CMV who are not required to always stop, failure to slow down at a railroad-highway grade crossing and check that tracks are clear of approaching train.
For individuals driving or operating a CMV who are not required to always stop, failure to stop before reaching tracks at a railroad-highway grade crossing when the tracks are not clear.
For individuals driving or operating a CMV who are always required to stop, failure to stop as required before driving onto railroad-highway grade crossing.
For all individuals driving or operating a CMV, failing to have sufficient space to drive completely through the railroad-highway grade crossing without stopping.
For all individuals driving or operating a CMV, failing to negotiate a railroad-highway grade crossing because of insufficient undercarriage clearance.
Failure to stop - basic rule at unsigned intersection or when entering roadway from private driveway, alley, etc.
Following improperly
Failure to leave sufficient distance for overtaking by other vehicles
Following emergency vehicle unlawfully
Following fire equipment unlawfully
Following too closely
Improper lane or location
Failure to keep in proper lane
Improper or erratic (unsafe) lane changes
Ran off road
Improper lane or location - crossover
Improper lane or location - crosswalk
Improper lane or location - entrance/exit ramp or way
Improper lane or location - in bicycle lane
Improper lane or location - in occupied lane
Improper lane or location - in HOV or restricted lane
Improper lane or location - limited access highway
Improper lane or location - median
Improper lane or location - on rail or streetcar tracks
Improper lane or location - on fire hose
Improper lane or location - oncoming traffic lane
Improper lane or location - road shoulder, ditch or sidewalk
Improper lane or location - slower vehicle lane
Improper lane or location - straddling center line(s) GImropurpop)er lane or location - traveling in turn (or center) lane
ACDCode.Abbreviation: IMP LOC CNTR IMP LOC TURN

ACDCode.Description: Improper lane or location - traveling in turn (or center) lane
Improper passing
Passing in violation of posted sign or pavement marking
Passing in violation of opposite directions restriction
Passing on wrong side
Passing on hill or curve
Passing school bus displaying warning not to pass
Passing where prohibited
Passing with insufficient distance or visibility
Inattentive/distracted, careless, or negligent driving or operating a motor vehicle
Careless driving or operating a motor vehicle
Inattentive/distractive driving or operating a motor vehicle
Negligent driving or operating a motor vehicle
Reckless driving or operating a motor vehicle
Texting while driving or operating a motor vehicle
Violating prohibition on using a hand-held mobile telephone or other electronic mobile device while driving or operating a motor vehicle
Failure to yield right of way (FTY ROW)
FTY ROW to animal rider or animal-drawn vehicle
FTY ROW to cyclist
FTY ROW to emergency vehicle (i.e., ambulance, fire equipment, police, etc.)
FTY ROW to funeral procession, procession or parade
FTY ROW to other vehicle
FTY ROW to overtaking vehicle
FTY ROW to pedestrian (includes handicapped or blind)
FTY ROW to school bus
FTY ROW at crosswalk
FTY ROW at rotary/roundabout/circular intersection
FTY ROW at stop sign
FTY ROW at traffic sign
FTY ROW at traffic signal
FTY ROW at unsigned intersection
FTY ROW at yield sign
FTY ROW when warning displayed on other vehicle SignFaTlY ( FRTOSW G wrohuepn) turning
FTY ROW when turning
Failure to use or improper signal
Failure to cancel directional signals
Failure to signal intention to pass
Failure to signal lane change or turn
Giving wrong signal
Improper turn
Improper method of turning
Improper position for turning
Making improper left turn
Making improper right turn
Making improper turn around (not U turn)
Making improper U turn
Driving wrong way
Driving wrong way at rotary/roundabout/circular intersection
Driving wrong way on divided highway
Driving wrong way on one way street or road
Driving on wrong side
Driving on wrong side of divided highway
Driving on wrong side of undivided street or road
Coasting (operating with gears disengaged)
Improper backing
Improper start from parked position
Unsafe operation
01-05 > Regulated or posted speed limit (detail optional)
06-10 > Regulated or posted speed limit (detail optional)
11-14 > Regulated or posted speed limit (detail optional)
Speeding 15 mph or more above regulated or posted speed limit (detail optional)
16-20 > Regulated or posted speed limit (detail optional)
21-25 > Regulated or posted speed limit (detail optional)
26-30 > Regulated or posted speed limit (detail optional)
31-35 > Regulated or posted speed limit (detail optional)
36-40 > Regulated or posted speed limit (detail optional)
41+ > Regulated or posted speed limit (detail optional)
01-10 > Regulated or posted speed limit (detail optional)
21-30 > Regulated or posted speed limit (detail optional)
31-40 > Regulated or posted speed limit (detail optional)
41+ > Regulated or posted speed limit (detail optional)
Speeding - Regulated or posted speed limit and actual speed (detail required)
Speeding
Prima Facie speed violation or driving too fast for conditions
Speed contest (racing) on road open to traffic
Speed less than minimum
Operating or driving a motor vehicle at erratic or suddenly changing speeds
Speeding on freeway ("wasting fuel")
Fleeing or evading police or roadblock
Resisting arrest while driving or operating a motor vehicle
Using a motor vehicle in connection with a felony (not traffic offense)
Using a motor vehicle in connection with a misdemeanor (not traffic offense)
Using a motor vehicle to aid and abet a felon
Vehicular assault
Vehicular homicide
Vehicular manslaughter
Negligent homicide while driving or operating a CMV
Causing a fatality through the negligent driving or operation of a CMV
Using a CMV in the commission of severe forms of trafficking in persons.
Illegal operation of emergency vehicle
Vehicular feticide (1st degree)
Vehicular feticide (2nd degree)
Violation resulting in fatal crash
Withdrawal resulting from one designated conviction
Suspended for driving or operating a CMV while disqualified for previous violations in a CMV
Withdrawal, Non-ACD violation
Accumulation of convictions (including point systems and/or being judged habitual offender or violator)
Suspended pending final disposition
Failed employer-directed drug test
Refusal to submit to an employer-directed drug test
Failure to surrender license or permit or otherwise comply with jurisdiction requirements
Failure to surrender hazmat endorsement as required by the USA PATRIOT Act
Parental consent withdrawn
Physical or mental disability
Physicians' or specialists' report recommended
Unable to pass DL test(s) or meet qualifications
Failure to make an appointment within 30 days of receiving notification that re-testing is necessary [when fraud is suspected]
Failure to retest or failure of scheduled test(s) [when fraud is suspected]
Two convictions for serious violations within three years
Three or more convictions for serious violations within three years
The accumulation of two or more convictions for major offenses
An additional conviction for a major offense after reinstatement
The accumulation of two convictions for out-of-service order general violations (violations not covered by W51) within 10 years

The accumulation of two convictions for out-of-service order violations within 10 years where the second is while operating a vehicle designed to transport 16 or more passengers, including the driver and/or transporting hazardous materials as defined in § 383.5
The accumulation of three or more convictions for out-of-service order violations within 10 years
The accumulation of two convictions for RRGC violations within 3 years.
The accumulation of three or more convictions for RRGC violations within years.
Imminent hazard
